scope: mobile and VR-friendly cytoplasm swimming controls

src/MainCellExperience/BuildMainCellExpe: Shifted Lighting and Atmosphere away from warm/yellow grading toward a cooler cyan-blue cytoplasm baseline.
Reduced bloom, exposure, glare, and blur so lighting remains more stable and less blown out.
Replaced warm/gold fill lights with lower-intensity cool cyan, blue, and purple fills.
Increased swim speed/acceleration response and enlarged touch controls for phone steering.
Kept gamepad and VR thumbstick input paths active for non-keyboard play.

studio_application.official_mcp_status: timed out; legacy bridge used successfully

remaining_risks: Phone steering still needs real device or Studio device-emulation play testing; desktop Studio cannot fully prove touch ergonomics.

The main-cell Blender package imports are still missing in the current Studio place, so the environment builder warns about missing main-cell packages. This pass focused on controls, lighting, and the current nucleus assembly.
The swim color grade is installed in the LocalScript and will appear only after Play starts.
next_recommended_phase: Run a short device-control review on phone and VR, then tune touch pad size, acceleration, and camera behavior from observed player movement.
